generated_by: Claude Code (CodemIE) — agentic ingestion of automated test files
scope: Automated UI tests only — 32 tests across 6 test files

stories.summary: Create a part with required fields only

description.summary: Enable a warehouse manager to register a new part by entering only the mandatory name and description via the InvenTree web UI.
description.general_description: The Part creation form must accept a submission containing only the two required fields (name and description). On success the modal closes, the new part appears in the parts list, and the part detail page correctly reflects the entered values.
description.business_value: Reduces friction for rapid inventory entry; testers and warehouse staff can register components without filling optional metadata.
preconditions: InvenTree is running and accessible at INVENTREE_URL
Browser is at the Parts category page (/web/part/category/index/parts)

scenarios.name: Create part — happy path
scenarios.given: an authenticated user on the Parts list page
scenarios.when: the user opens the Add Part menu, enters a name and description, and clicks Submit
scenarios.then: the modal closes, the part appears in the list, and the part detail page shows the correct name

acceptance_criteria: Form accepts name + description with no other fields filled
Part appears in parts list immediately after creation
Part detail page shows the exact name entered
Part is accessible via API GET /api/part/{pk}/

stories.summary: Create a part with an Internal Part Number (IPN)

description.summary: Allow a procurement manager to assign an IPN to a new part at creation time.
description.general_description: The IPN (Internal Part Number) is an optional unique identifier. When provided at creation the value must be stored and displayed on the part detail page.
description.business_value: Supports internal numbering schemes required by procurement and ERP integration workflows.
preconditions: No existing part with the same IPN

scenarios.name: Create part with IPN
scenarios.given: an authenticated user on the Parts list page
scenarios.when: the user fills name, description, and IPN then submits
scenarios.then: the part is created and the IPN is visible on the part detail page

acceptance_criteria: IPN field accepts alphanumeric values
Created part appears in the parts list
Part detail page displays the IPN value

stories.summary: Duplicate an existing part via the action menu

description.summary: Allow a parts librarian to create a copy of an existing part with a new name via the detail page action menu.
description.general_description: The Duplicate Part action pre-fills all fields from the source part. The user can rename before saving. The result is a new independent part record.
description.business_value: Reduces data-entry time when creating similar parts; avoids re-typing shared specifications.
preconditions: At least one part exists in InvenTree

scenarios.name: Duplicate part and rename
scenarios.given: an authenticated user on a part detail page
scenarios.when: the user selects Duplicate from the actions menu, changes the name, and submits
scenarios.then: a new part with the given name is created and findable via API

acceptance_criteria: Duplicate option is available in the part actions menu (action-menu-part-actions-duplicate)
Pre-filled modal allows renaming
New part is created and findable via API with the new name
Original part is unchanged

stories.summary: Validation error shown when part name is missing

description.summary: Prevent creation of unnamed parts by showing an inline validation error when the Name field is left empty.
description.general_description: Name is mandatory. Submitting the create part form without a name must trigger a field-level validation error. The form must remain open and no part record must be created.
description.business_value: Data quality — every part in the inventory must have a human-readable name.

scenarios.name: Submit form without name
scenarios.given: an open Part creation modal
scenarios.when: the user fills description but leaves name empty and clicks Submit
scenarios.then: an inline validation error appears on the Name field and the modal stays open

acceptance_criteria: Name field shows inline error on submit-without-name
Modal remains open after invalid submission
No part record is created (API returns 0 results for an empty search)

stories.summary: Validation error shown for duplicate IPN

description.summary: Prevent duplicate IPN values by rejecting a creation form that specifies an IPN already assigned to another part.
description.general_description: IPN uniqueness is a system constraint. When a user enters an IPN that is already in use, the form must show an error and must not create a second part with the same IPN.
description.business_value: Maintains IPN uniqueness, which is essential for integration with ERP and procurement systems.
preconditions: A part with IPN 'DUP-IPN-005' already exists

scenarios.name: Submit form with duplicate IPN
scenarios.given: a part with IPN 'DUP-IPN-005' already exists
scenarios.when: the user creates another part with the same IPN
scenarios.then: an error is shown on the IPN field and no new part is created

acceptance_criteria: IPN field shows an error when a duplicate is submitted
Modal stays open
Only the original part with that IPN exists after the attempt

stories.summary: Part name length boundary — 100 chars accepted, 101 rejected

description.summary: Enforce the 100-character maximum name length: a 100-character name must be accepted and a 101-character name must be rejected with a validation error.
description.general_description: The InvenTree API enforces a 100-character name limit. The UI must reflect this constraint with appropriate feedback.
description.business_value: Prevents oversized data from causing API/database errors and enforces consistent part naming.

scenarios.name: 100-char name accepted
scenarios.given: part creation modal is open
scenarios.when: user enters exactly 100 characters as the name and submits
scenarios.then: part is created successfully and appears in the list

scenarios.name: 101-char name rejected
scenarios.given: part creation modal is open
scenarios.when: user enters 101 characters as the name and submits
scenarios.then: validation error is shown and no part is created

acceptance_criteria: 100-character name: form submits successfully, part appears in list
101-character name: inline validation error shown, modal stays open

stories.summary: Create a part assigned to a category

description.summary: Allow a parts librarian to assign a new part to an existing category during creation using the category dropdown.
description.general_description: The category dropdown (react-select) on the create part modal accepts an existing category name. The part should be findable under that category after creation.
description.business_value: Ensures parts are correctly organised into the hierarchy from the start, avoiding orphaned parts.
preconditions: A category named 'UI-P-008 Test Category' exists

scenarios.name: Assign category during part creation
scenarios.given: part creation modal open, at least one category exists
scenarios.when: user fills name, description, selects the category, and submits
scenarios.then: part is created with the category assignment persisted

acceptance_criteria: Category react-select dropdown filters and allows selection by typing
Part is created and visible in the parts list
Category assignment is visible on the part detail page

stories.summary: Edit a part's name via the UI action menu

description.summary: Allow a warehouse manager to rename an existing part through the Edit action on the part detail page.
description.general_description: The Edit action opens a modal pre-filled with current part values. The user changes the name and saves. The update must be immediately visible on the detail page.
description.business_value: Enables correction of naming errors post-creation without requiring API access.
preconditions: A part exists with a known PK

scenarios.name: Rename part via edit modal
scenarios.given: authenticated user on a part detail page
scenarios.when: user clicks Edit, clears the name, enters a new name, and clicks Submit
scenarios.then: the part detail page reflects the new name immediately

acceptance_criteria: Edit modal pre-fills current name
New name is accepted on submit
Part detail page immediately shows the new name
API GET returns the new name

stories.summary: Delete an inactive part via the UI

description.summary: Allow a warehouse manager to permanently delete a deactivated part through the detail page action menu.
description.general_description: InvenTree enforces a soft-delete pattern: parts must be deactivated (active=false) before deletion. The UI Delete menu item is disabled for active parts. After deactivation the item becomes enabled and clicking it shows a confirmation modal.
description.business_value: Keeps the parts catalogue clean by allowing removal of obsolete components through the UI.
preconditions: A part exists with a known PK
Part has been deactivated via API PATCH (active=false)

scenarios.name: Delete inactive part
scenarios.given: authenticated user on the detail page of an inactive part
scenarios.when: user opens the actions menu, clicks Delete, and confirms in the modal
scenarios.then: the part is deleted and the API returns 404 for that PK

acceptance_criteria: Delete menu item is disabled for active parts
After deactivation (page reload) Delete item is enabled
Confirmation modal shows Delete button
After confirmation modal closes the part is gone (API returns 404)

stories.summary: Part attribute flags reveal corresponding detail tabs

description.summary: Verify that setting Assembly, Component, Purchaseable, and Template flags each causes the corresponding tab to appear on the part detail page.
description.general_description: InvenTree conditionally shows tabs based on part configuration. Assembly shows BOM, Component shows Used In, Purchaseable shows Suppliers, Template shows Variants.
description.business_value: Ensures the UI surface area matches the part's actual capabilities — prevents user confusion about missing functionality.
preconditions: Part created with the relevant attribute flag via API

scenarios.name: Assembly → BOM tab
scenarios.given: part with assembly=true
scenarios.when: user navigates to detail page
scenarios.then: BOM tab is visible

scenarios.name: Component → Used In tab
scenarios.given: part with component=true
scenarios.when: user navigates to detail page
scenarios.then: Used In / Allocated tab is visible

scenarios.name: Purchaseable → Suppliers tab
scenarios.given: part with purchaseable=true
scenarios.when: user navigates to detail page
scenarios.then: Suppliers tab is visible

scenarios.name: Template → Variants tab
scenarios.given: part with is_template=true
scenarios.when: user navigates to detail page
scenarios.then: Variants tab is visible

acceptance_criteria: assembly=true → BOM tab present in tab list
component=true → Used In tab present in tab list
purchaseable=true → Suppliers tab present in tab list
is_template=true → Variants tab present in tab list

stories.summary: Multiple part attribute flags saved simultaneously

description.summary: Verify that multiple attribute flags (Assembly+Component, Purchaseable+Salable) can be set simultaneously and all corresponding tabs appear.
description.general_description: Parts may have multiple attributes active at once. The UI must correctly show all relevant tabs when multiple flags are set.
description.business_value: Real inventory parts often have multiple classifications (an assembly that is also purchaseable, for example).
preconditions: Part created with multiple attribute flags via API

scenarios.name: Assembly+Component
scenarios.given: part with assembly=true and component=true
scenarios.when: user views detail page
scenarios.then: BOM tab appears

scenarios.name: Purchaseable+Salable
scenarios.given: part with purchaseable=true and salable=true
scenarios.when: user views detail page
scenarios.then: Suppliers tab appears

acceptance_criteria: assembly=true + component=true → BOM tab visible
purchaseable=true + salable=true → Suppliers tab visible

stories.summary: BOM tab is hidden for non-Assembly parts

description.summary: Verify the BOM tab does not appear on parts with Assembly=false, keeping the UI uncluttered.
description.general_description: Negative assertion: the BOM tab must be absent when assembly is not set. This confirms conditional tab rendering is working bidirectionally.
description.business_value: Prevents user confusion — showing a BOM tab on a non-assembly part would imply structure where none exists.

scenarios.name: BOM tab absent
scenarios.given: part with assembly=false
scenarios.when: user navigates to detail page
scenarios.then: BOM tab is not in the tab list

acceptance_criteria: BOM tab selector [aria-label='...bom...'] is not visible on a non-assembly part detail page

stories.summary: Part parameters: add, verify tab visibility, and boundary values

description.summary: Verify that parameters can be added to a part via the UI Parameters tab, the tab is always visible, and the value field accepts positive, zero, and negative numbers.
description.general_description: Parameters tab is always visible. Using the Add Parameter action, a user selects a parameter template and enters a value. Boundary values (positive, zero, negative) must all be accepted.
description.business_value: Parameters carry technical specifications. Wide value acceptance (including zero and negative) ensures all physical measurements can be recorded.
preconditions: A part exists with a known PK
A parameter template exists or is created via API

scenarios.name: Add parameter with value
scenarios.given: part detail page, Parameters tab active
scenarios.when: user adds parameter with template and value '10000'
scenarios.then: parameter appears in the parameters table

scenarios.name: Parameters tab always visible
scenarios.given: any part detail page
scenarios.when: user views the tab list
scenarios.then: Parameters tab is present

scenarios.name: Positive value accepted
scenarios.given: parameters form open
scenarios.when: user enters '100'
scenarios.then: parameter is saved and visible

scenarios.name: Zero value accepted
scenarios.given: parameters form open
scenarios.when: user enters '0'
scenarios.then: parameter is saved and visible

scenarios.name: Negative value accepted
scenarios.given: parameters form open
scenarios.when: user enters '-1'
scenarios.then: parameter is saved and visible

acceptance_criteria: Add Parameter modal accepts a template selection and a free-text value
Saved parameter name and value are visible on the Parameters tab
Values 100, 0, and -1 are all accepted without validation errors

stories.summary: Create a root-level part category via the UI

description.summary: Allow a parts librarian to create a top-level (root) category through the category index page.
description.general_description: Root categories have no parent. They are created from the category index page using the Add Category button.
description.business_value: Top-level categories form the primary organisational structure of the parts catalogue.

scenarios.name: Create root category
scenarios.given: authenticated user on category index
scenarios.when: user clicks Add Category, fills name and description, submits
scenarios.then: category appears in the list

acceptance_criteria: New root category appears in the category list after creation
Category is accessible via API GET /api/part/category/

stories.summary: Create a child subcategory under a parent category

description.summary: Allow a parts librarian to create a subcategory nested under an existing parent via the parent category page.
description.general_description: Subcategories inherit the parent's context. The creation form is accessed from the parent category's subcategories tab.
description.business_value: Enables multi-level taxonomy for organising complex parts inventories.

scenarios.name: Create child category
scenarios.given: authenticated user on parent category page
scenarios.when: user creates a subcategory with a name
scenarios.then: child category appears in the page and is linked to the parent

acceptance_criteria: Child category visible in the page after creation
Parent–child relationship persisted (API confirms parent field)

stories.summary: Navigate a multi-level category hierarchy using breadcrumbs

description.summary: Verify that navigating into a 3-level category hierarchy shows breadcrumbs containing ancestor names.
description.general_description: InvenTree shows breadcrumbs when inside a category. For a grandparent → parent → child structure, navigating to the child should show at least one ancestor in the breadcrumb bar.
description.business_value: Breadcrumbs are essential navigation aids in deep hierarchies — without them users lose context.

scenarios.name: Navigate to child, check breadcrumbs
scenarios.given: 3-level category hierarchy
scenarios.when: user opens the child category page
scenarios.then: breadcrumb bar shows at least one ancestor name

acceptance_criteria: Breadcrumb text contains 'UI-P-036 Grandparent' or 'UI-P-036 Parent'
Page renders without error

stories.summary: Access the parametric table view from a category page

description.summary: Verify that the parametric table view is accessible from a category detail page.
description.general_description: Each category page provides access to a parametric table that shows parts as rows and their parameters as columns.
description.business_value: Parametric search is a key value-add of InvenTree for engineering teams comparing component specifications.

scenarios.name: Access parametric view
scenarios.given: authenticated user on a category detail page
scenarios.when: user views the page
scenarios.then: page loads without error and parametric functionality is accessible

acceptance_criteria: Category detail page loads without error at /web/part/category/{pk}/

stories.summary: Delete an empty category via the UI

description.summary: Allow a parts librarian to delete an empty category through the category detail page.
description.general_description: Empty categories can be deleted from the UI. The delete action is available in the category actions menu.
description.business_value: Keeps the category tree clean and prevents accumulation of unused organisational structures.

scenarios.name: Delete empty category
scenarios.given: authenticated user on an empty category detail page
scenarios.when: user opens category actions, clicks Delete, and confirms
scenarios.then: category is deleted (API returns 404)

acceptance_criteria: Delete option available in category actions menu
Confirmation modal appears
After confirmation: API returns 404 for the deleted category PK

stories.summary: Visual regression baseline for Part detail page

description.summary: Capture and maintain a pixel-level baseline screenshot of the Part detail page to automatically detect CSS and layout regressions.
description.general_description: D1 differentiator: PIL/numpy screenshot comparison. On first run (or UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=true) a baseline is saved to snapshots/part-detail.png. On subsequent runs the live screenshot is compared against the baseline with a 10% mean pixel-diff tolerance.
description.business_value: Catches visual regressions that DOM-based assertions miss — layout shifts, font changes, component mis-rendering.
preconditions: A part exists with component=true and purchaseable=true
Baseline exists in automation/ui/snapshots/ or UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=true

scenarios.name: Capture or compare baseline
scenarios.given: part detail page loaded to networkidle
scenarios.when: screenshot is taken with dynamic elements hidden
scenarios.then: pixel diff against baseline is within 10% threshold

acceptance_criteria: Screenshot captured after networkidle
Dynamic elements (timestamps) masked before capture
Pixel diff ≤ 10% threshold OR baseline is saved on first run

stories.summary: Visual regression baseline for Part creation form

description.summary: Capture and maintain a baseline screenshot of the Part creation modal dialog.
description.general_description: The create part modal is a key UI surface. Any layout change (field order, button position, form width) should be caught by this visual test.
description.business_value: Prevents silent regressions in form layout that could confuse users or hide fields.

scenarios.name: Capture create modal baseline
scenarios.given: create part modal is open and fully rendered
scenarios.when: screenshot taken
scenarios.then: pixel diff within 10% threshold

acceptance_criteria: Modal is fully visible before screenshot ([role='dialog'] is visible)
Pixel diff ≤ 10%

stories.summary: Visual regression baseline for Category list view

description.summary: Capture and maintain a full-page baseline screenshot of a category detail view.
description.general_description: Full-page screenshot of a category containing a test category created via API. Covers the category header, tab bar, parts table, and breadcrumbs.
description.business_value: Category views are high-traffic pages; regressions here directly impact day-to-day warehouse operations.

scenarios.name: Capture category page baseline
scenarios.given: category detail page loaded to networkidle
scenarios.when: full-page screenshot taken with dynamic content masked
scenarios.then: pixel diff within 10% threshold

acceptance_criteria: Full-page screenshot captured after networkidle
Pixel diff ≤ 10%

stories.summary: Visual regression baseline for part with all attributes enabled

description.summary: Capture a baseline of a part that has Assembly, Component, Trackable, Purchaseable, and Salable all enabled — the maximum tab-count configuration.
description.general_description: A part with all relevant flags shows the most tabs (BOM, Used In, Suppliers, etc.). This configuration is most likely to expose tab-layout regressions.
description.business_value: Catches regressions triggered by specific attribute combinations that wouldn't surface in tests with fewer attributes.
preconditions: Part with all flags enabled via API

scenarios.name: Capture all-attributes part baseline
scenarios.given: part detail page with all attribute flags set
scenarios.when: screenshot taken after networkidle
scenarios.then: pixel diff within 10% threshold

acceptance_criteria: Screenshot captured after networkidle with dynamic content masked
Pixel diff ≤ 10%

stories.summary: End-to-end part lifecycle: create → parameters → stock → category verification

description.summary: Validate the complete InvenTree parts workflow in a single end-to-end flow: create a categorised part via UI, add a parameter, create a stock item, verify the part in the category view, and assert a final visual baseline.
description.general_description: This cross-functional test exercises every major UI surface in sequence. It is the primary integration confidence test for the UI layer. It also includes a D1 visual snapshot at the final state.
description.business_value: Provides single-test confidence that the entire UI workflow — from part creation to stocked inventory — is functioning as an integrated system.
preconditions: Category 'XF Test Category' can be created via API
Parameter template 'XF Resistance' can be created via API

scenarios.name: Full cross-functional flow
scenarios.given: authenticated user, category and parameter template created via API
scenarios.when: user: (1) creates part via UI in that category, (2) adds parameter via Parameters tab, (3) adds stock item via Stock tab, (4) navigates to category Parts sub-tab
scenarios.then: (1) part visible in list, (2) parameter visible on part page, (3) stock quantity 25 visible, (4) part visible in category view, (5) visual snapshot within threshold

acceptance_criteria: Part created via UI is findable via API
Parameter 'XF Resistance' with value '4700' visible on part detail
Stock tab shows quantity 25 after stock creation
Part visible in category's Parts sub-tab
Final visual snapshot passes 10% pixel diff threshold
